deployed_via: hud deploy (POST /v2/builds/trigger, server-side AWS CodeBuild + ECR push), authenticated with the trusted-context HUD_API_KEY
non_destructive: Versions are append-only immutable builds. v5 is the new latest_build; v1-v4 are retained (total builds = 5). Existing runs/traces are not invalidated.

version_history.v3: First hardened publish (build 3bc11838). A post-publish bug hunt found v3 still bypassable: because the agent runs as root, a force-pass conftest.py planted directly in /tests/task_assets defeated the grader (PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD does not disable conftest discovery, and the v3 digest gate only covered test_outputs.py). Superseded by v4; do not rely on v3.
version_history.v4: Second hardened publish (build d7b8043a). Blocks the recorded conftest hack + /tests tampering, but a parallel bug-hunt found it still bypassable by an in-process exit-code hijack (/app/query.py: atexit -> os._exit(0) overrides pytest's failing exit code). Superseded by v5.
version_history.v5: Build 0290bbb9. Grades the verdict from pytest's JUnit XML in a SECOND process that never imports the agent solution, so the exit-code hijack is blocked (re-proven in v2-deploy-proof.json: exit_code_hijack_blocked). KNOWN RESIDUAL (see v2-security-limitations.json): the suite still imports the solution in-process, so a determined in-process monkeypatch / JUnit-XML overwrite / answer-hardcoding can still bypass; a complete fix is an out-of-process solution sandbox + hidden test data (the harden-v0/Harbor reward-file + SEPARATE-verifier convention).
